mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining federal funds rates indicates strong consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.04, or forward returns are not significantly positive when both retail sales growth and rate declines align.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds historical peaks.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly signal economic expansion and higher corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative 20-day forward returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 212.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 212.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and declines in the unemployment rate indicate a synchronized recovery in residential investment and labor demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction term predictive accuracy falls below Rank IC 0.03 over rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% during a monetary easing cycle confirms economic resilience while low discount rates amplify equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% during easing cycles fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the mechanism direction flips to negative when real interest rates turn positive.
